mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflects a robust and expanding economy, leading to higher corporate earnings expectations and positive equity returns. This is a persistent, always-on signal of economic health.
failure_condition: The signal's walk-forward rank information coefficient (IC) remains below 0.03 over any rolling 3-year period when using a 3-month rate of change as the signal transformation.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate is near the zero lower bound, monetary policy is maximally accommodative, lowering the discount rate for future cash flows and encouraging investment in risk assets like equities.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are not statistically significantly higher (p > 0.05) during periods when FEDFUNDS < 0.25% compared to periods when it is higher.

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales (RSAFS) are growing but the unemployment rate (UNRATE) is also rising, it indicates that consumer spending is disconnected from labor market health, likely fueled by temporary factors like stimulus or credit. This unsustainable divergence is a negative signal for future economic activity and earnings.
failure_condition: Instances where RSAFS 6-month growth is positive while UNRATE 3-month change is > 0.2 are not followed by negative S&P 500 returns more than 50% of the time over a full business cycle.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-recessionary recovery, accelerating industrial production (INDPRO) signals that the supply side of the economy is healing, leading to improved corporate profitability and capital investment, which drives equity prices higher.
failure_condition: The correlation between 6-month changes in INDPRO and 60-day forward S&P 500 returns is negative or statistically insignificant during periods identified as economic recoveries.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate above a structural threshold (e.g., 6.5%) indicates significant slack in the labor market, which suppresses wage growth, consumer demand, and corporate pricing power, leading to negative forward returns for the S&P 500.
failure_condition: The average 20-day forward S&P 500 return is not statistically lower (p > 0.05) in months where UNRATE > 6.5% compared to months where it is below the threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Persistently rising inflation (CPIAUCSL) while the central bank holds policy rates (FEDFUNDS) low creates deeply negative real yields. This condition forces capital into riskier, real assets like equities to preserve purchasing power, driving prices up.
failure_condition: In periods where FEDFUNDS < 1.0%, the correlation between the 6-month change in CPIAUCSL and 60-day forward S&P 500 returns is negative or insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Positive quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP (GDPC1) is the broadest signal of economic expansion. This directly translates into higher aggregate corporate revenues and earnings, supporting higher equity valuations over a medium-term horizon.
failure_condition: The beta of 60-day forward S&P 500 returns to the quarter-over-quarter percentage change in GDPC1 is not positive and statistically significant over a full business cycle.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month decline in housing starts (HOUST) is a high-frequency leading indicator of a slowdown in construction and related sectors, signaling weakening consumer confidence and tighter financial conditions, which negatively impacts short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: The hit rate of the signal (correctly predicting negative 5-day returns) is less than 55% over a 10-year backtest.
